practical: Standard full metal jacket. Stable flight, good penetration, poor yaw or fragmentation.
visual: Smooth copper tip with full metal coating, uniform shine.

practical: Jacketed hollow point. Expands on impact. Devastating to soft targets, bad vs armor.
visual: Indented nose cavity, usually exposed lead with copper rim.

practical: Armor piercing. Hardened core penetrates protection layers. Overpenetrates flesh.
visual: Black or steel tip, denser core, often matte finish or segmented ring.

practical: Lead-tipped bullet. Expands irregularly, causes fragmentation. Inconsistent.
visual: Exposed soft lead nose with copper base jacket, dull tip.

practical: Glows in flight. Easy to trace. Minimal ballistic difference.
visual: Color-tipped bullet, often red or orange with visible streaks.

practical: Ignites flammable targets. Not legal for common use. Penetrates with added flame.
visual: Silver or blue tip with burn marks or discoloration.

practical: Shatters on impact. Minimal penetration. Used for training or air marshals.
visual: Powdery tip, fragile composite look, matte light finish.
